The configurations operate as structurally analogous vehicles of trans-tradition reactionary cooperation under different theological banners.

### I. The Christian Right International Coordination Architecture

The Christian Right international coordination architecture serves as the principal vehicle of trans-Christian-tradition reactionary cooperation in the contemporary period. The architecture has five principal nodes: (1) the World Congress of Families (WCF) as the principal umbrella for Christian Right international networking from 1997. (2) the Alliance Defending Freedom (ADF) as the principal Christian Right legal-litigation vehicle operating in 90+ countries through ADF International. (3) CitizenGO as the principal Christian Right online-petition and digital-mobilisation vehicle from 2013. (4) the broader US-based Christian Right ecosystem including the Family Research Council. Focus on the Family, National Organization for Marriage, Family Watch International, and the Heritage Foundation; (5) the European Christian Right vehicles including Agenda Europe, the Political Network for Values, and Vatican-adjacent advocacy networks.

**The World Congress of Families (WCF) founded 1997.** The WCF was founded 1997 in a meeting in Moscow between American academic and Reagan-era National Commission on Children appointee Allan Carlson (Howard Center for Family, Religion and Society, Rockford Illinois) and Russian intellectuals Anatoly Antonov and Viktor Medkov. The architecture: WCF defines its objective as the promotion of "the natural family" — defined as "the union of a man and a woman in a lifelong covenant of marriage"; opposition to same-sex marriage, abortion, and "gender ideology" frameworks; institutional founding meeting in Prague 1997 attracting over 700 participants. The WCF Congresses 1997-2024: Prague (1997, founding), Geneva (1999), Mexico City (2004), Warsaw (2007), Amsterdam (2009), Madrid (2012), Sydney (2013), Salt Lake City (2015), Tbilisi Georgia (2016), Budapest (2017), Verona (2019), and subsequent annual events including gatherings during the post-2020 COVID period. The WCF leadership: Allan Carlson as founding International Secretary; Brian Brown (also National Organization for Marriage founder) as President from 2016. The WCF is the principal umbrella for Christian Right international networking — and as the vehicle through which US Christian Right figures coordinate with European, Latin American, African, and Russian counterparts.

**WCF designation by the Southern Poverty Law Center.** Per the Southern Poverty Law Center reporting: WCF was added to the SPLC list of organisations designated as anti-LGBT hate groups in February 2014 — citing the WCF involvement with Russian 2013 anti-LGBT propaganda law and opposition to LGBTQ rights internationally. The architecture operates with consequences for WCF reputational positioning — the Human Rights Campaign characterisation of WCF as "one of the most influential groups in America promoting and coordinating the exportation of anti-LGBT bigotry, ideology, and legislation abroad" per the Human Rights Campaign reporting. The WCF response: denial of anti-LGBT positioning while advocacy for "natural family" framework continues.

**The Alliance Defending Freedom (ADF) founded 1994.** Founded 1994 by James Dobson (Focus on the Family founder), Bill Bright (Campus Crusade for Christ founder), Alan Sears (former Reagan-era Meese Commission director on pornography), and around 30 other Christian Right leaders. The architecture: ADF serves as the principal Christian Right legal-litigation vehicle in the United States; litigation portfolio includes Burwell v. Hobby Lobby (2014, holding closely-held corporations could refuse to provide contraception coverage under ACA on religious-liberty grounds), Masterpiece Cakeshop v. Colorado Civil Rights Commission (2018, regarding same-sex wedding cake refusal), Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization (2022, overturning Roe v. Wade), and 303 Creative LLC v. Elenis (2023, regarding wedding-website refusal to serve same-sex couples). ADF reported gross revenues over $48 million for 2014 per Political Research Associates analysis; massive expansion of ADF resources across the post-2014 period including growth into the post-2020 period. ADF was originally called the Alliance Defense Fund (founding 1994); the name change to Alliance Defending Freedom occurred 2012.

**ADF International (ADFI).** The international wing of ADF operates in 90+ countries per Reactionary International database; UN special consultative status (Economic and Social Council); advisory status with the European Union and the Organization of American States. ADFI is the principal vehicle through which US Christian Right legal strategies are exported globally — pattern of taking US-tested legal strategies, adapting them to foreign jurisdictions, and advocacy for adoption by foreign legislatures and courts. Per Global Project Against Hate and Extremism reporting (May 2022): ADFI provides funding to WCF, the Political Network for Values, and Agenda Europe; ADF cooperation with former Vice President Mike Pence and former Attorney General Jeff Sessions during the Trump administration 2017-2021.

**CitizenGO founded 2013.** Founded 2013 in Madrid by Ignacio Arsuaga — Spanish Catholic activist and founder of the vehicle HazteOir (founded 2001). The architecture: CitizenGO serves as the principal Christian Right online-petition and digital-mobilisation vehicle globally; reach in 50+ countries with petitions in 12+ languages; offices in 15+ countries across Africa, Latin America, Oceania, and Europe; the board includes Brian Brown (WCF), Sharon Slater (Family Watch International), and Luca Volonte (former Italian parliamentarian and anti-sexual-and-reproductive-health-rights activist). The CitizenGO petition portfolio: hundreds of petitions targeting LGBTQ rights, abortion access, World Health Organization positions, and sexual-and-reproductive-health-rights frameworks. Post-7 October 2023: CitizenGO petitions increasingly focused on pro-Israel and anti-protest positions per Reactionary International database.

**The "bus of freedom" campaign 2017.** Per ISD Global December 2021 dispatch: CitizenGO/HazteOir 2017 "bus of freedom" campaign — bus painted with the slogan "boys have penises, girls have vulvas, don't be fooled" — toured Spain in 2017 producing controversy and model for subsequent CitizenGO transphobic-campaign operations globally. The architecture is illustration of CitizenGO methodology — attention-grabbing stunts producing media coverage producing fundraising and reach amplification.

**The broader US-based Christian Right ecosystem.** The architecture: Family Research Council (founded 1981 by James Dobson, Washington DC headquarters); Focus on the Family (founded 1977 by James Dobson, Colorado Springs headquarters); National Organization for Marriage (founded 2007 by Maggie Gallagher and Robert George, led by Brian Brown); Family Watch International (led by Sharon Slater); Heritage Foundation (founded 1973 by Paul Weyrich, Joseph Coors, and Edwin Feulner; Project 2025 published April 2023 as the blueprint for Trump second-term governance). The architecture operates as ecosystem — cooperation across organisational boundaries through shared leadership (multiple figures serving on multiple boards), cross-funding, and cooperation on advocacy campaigns.

**Agenda Europe.** The architecture: Vatican-inspired professional advocacy network active across the European Union from around 2012; anti-gender-ideology advocacy at EU institutions; cooperation with ADF International and CitizenGO per investigative journalism reporting. Agenda Europe is illustration of adaptation of Christian Right methodology to European terrain — emphasis on legal-litigation strategies and cooperation with sympathetic European parliamentarians and European officials.

**The Political Network for Values (PNfV).** Founded 2014 as a parliamentary network advocating "Christian values" across Latin America, Eastern Europe, and beyond. The architecture: the Mexico Declaration 2022 — signed by 670 lawmakers from 18 Latin American countries; opposition to OAS and Inter-American Commission on Human Rights resolutions on abortion-related issues, right-to-life, family, and freedom-of-expression questions; ADF financial support per Ciper Chile investigative reporting. The PNfV is illustration of Christian Right parliamentary-network methodology — cultivation of sympathetic parliamentarians across multiple countries producing coordinated declarations and opposition to documented regional human-rights frameworks.

**The Christian Right advocacy in Uganda 2023.** Per Global Project Against Hate and Extremism reporting: involvement of US Christian Right legal organisations including ADF International advocacy in support of Uganda's Anti-Homosexuality Act — signed by President Yoweri Museveni on 29 May 2023, providing the death penalty for "aggravated homosexuality" provisions; Uganda's Constitutional Court partial decision April 2024 upholding most of the law; pattern of US Christian Right advocacy producing foreign legislative-judicial outcomes harmful to LGBTQ persons.

**The the December 2025 Chile presidential election.** Per Global Project Against Hate and Extremism reporting (December 2025): the Chilean presidential election won by José Antonio Kast (Republican Party) with ADF Academy support including ADF financial support for the Mexico Declaration 2022 promotion; CitizenGo intervention through "illegal advertising" support for Kast per El Ciudadano reporting; ADF Academy paid Kast's airfare and accommodation in Maui Hawaii to attend an event sponsored by figures. The architecture is evidence of Christian Right capacity to influence Latin American electoral outcomes through financial and advocacy support to sympathetic candidates.

The summary of § I: the Christian Right international coordination architecture operates through the World Congress of Families (founded 1997), Alliance Defending Freedom (founded 1994) with ADF International in 90+ countries, CitizenGO (founded 2013) with 50+ countries reach, and the broader US-based Christian Right ecosystem (Family Research Council, Focus on the Family, NOM, Family Watch International, Heritage Foundation). The configuration operates with cooperation across organisational boundaries through shared leadership, cross-funding, and cooperation on advocacy campaigns including the Uganda Anti-Homosexuality Act, the Mexico Declaration 2022, the December 2025 Chile election, and multiple other outcomes harmful to LGBTQ persons and reproductive rights globally. The architecture operates alongside the Russian Orthodox international coordination dossiered in § II — and operates with overlapping cooperation across confessional boundaries dossiered in § III.

---


### II. The Russian Orthodox International Coordination

The Russian Orthodox international coordination architecture is the principal vehicle of post-Soviet Russian Orthodox international networking. The architecture has five principal nodes: (1) the Moscow Patriarchate global outreach as dossiered in Chapter 17. (2) the Konstantin Malofeev network operating across Russian Orthodox oligarchic and ideological vehicles. (3) the Alexander Dugin ideological framework operating through the "Fourth Political Theory" vehicle. (4) the Vladimir Yakunin network operating through the Dialogue of Civilizations vehicle. (5) the Russkiy Mir Foundation and World Russian People's Council as the Russian-state-affiliated vehicles of Russian-cultural and Russian-Orthodox international promotion.

**The Moscow Patriarchate global outreach.** Cross-reference Chapter 17. The architecture: Moscow Patriarchate serves as the principal Russian Orthodox vehicle with post-2009 Patriarch Kirill positioning of the Russian Orthodox Church as vehicle of Russian-state geopolitical-cultural influence globally; global presence through Moscow Patriarchate ecclesiastical exarchates and dioceses in multiple countries; Russian Orthodox Church Outside Russia (ROCOR) reunification with Moscow Patriarchate in 2007; cooperation with Russian state vehicles including Russian Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Russian Federal Security Service (FSB) per investigative reporting. The Moscow Patriarchate position post-February 2022: Patriarch Kirill support for Russian military operations in Ukraine including framing of the operation as "metaphysical struggle" and "holy war" — producing the rupture with the Ecumenical Patriarchate of Constantinople and multiple other Orthodox bodies (cross-reference Chapter 17 § III).

**Konstantin Malofeev (born 3 July 1974 Pushchino).** Operates as the principal contemporary Russian Orthodox oligarch and financial vehicle of Russian Orthodox-aligned international advocacy. The architecture: Malofeev founded Marshall Capital Partners private equity firm 2005; founder of Tsargrad Media (Tsargrad TV launched 2015) — Russian Orthodox-traditionalist media vehicle modelled in part on the Fox News model per analyst reporting; financial support for international anti-LGBTQ, traditionalist, and far-right networks per investigative journalism; the US Department of the Treasury sanctions on Malofeev December 2014 (for financial support of Russian military operations in eastern Ukraine 2014); the US Department of the Treasury sanctions in 2022 (for continuing financial support of Russian military operations); the US Department of Justice indictment April 2022 against Malofeev for documented sanctions evasion including financial transfers through intermediaries. Malofeev's role: financier of Russian-Orthodox-aligned networking including financial relationships with WCF (cross-reference § I) and financial relationships with European far-right vehicles per the Bureau of Investigative Journalism September 2022 reporting.

**Alexander Dugin (born 7 January 1962 Moscow).** Russian political philosopher operating as the principal contemporary Russian Orthodox-traditionalist ideological architect. The architecture: academic career at Lomonosov Moscow State University; positions including head of the Centre for Conservative Studies at Moscow State University until removal in 2014; publication of *The Fourth Political Theory* (2009) — a framework articulating post-liberal, post-Communist, post-Fascist political philosophy combining Russian Orthodox traditionalism, a Eurasianist geopolitical framework, and a critique of Western liberal-democratic framework; positions on Russian geopolitical priorities including advocacy for Russian intervention in Ukraine, Georgia, and the Caucasus from the post-Soviet period onward; relationship with Russian state figures including connection with Russian Federal Security Service per analyst reporting (relationship contested). Dugin's international reach: relationships with European far-right vehicles including connections with Marine Le Pen vehicle, Steve Bannon vehicle, Brazilian figures including former President Jair Bolsonaro's circle, and multiple other international figures across Europe, the Americas, and Asia. Dugin's position post-August 2022: daughter Darya Dugina (born 1992) killed in car bombing in Moscow 20 August 2022 — attribution to Ukrainian vehicles per Russian FSB statement; consequences including intensification of Dugin's anti-Ukrainian and anti-Western positions.

**Vladimir Yakunin (born 30 June 1948 Melenki Vladimir Oblast).** Former Russian Railways president (2005-2015) operating as the principal Russian Orthodox-aligned figure of post-Soviet international Orthodox-traditionalist networking. The architecture: Yakunin operated as Russian Railways president 2005-2015 producing wealth and connections; founder of Dialogue of Civilizations vehicle 2002 (founded with Indian businessman Jagdish Kapur and US businessman Nicholas Papanicolaou); Dialogue of Civilizations headquarters relocated to Berlin 2016 — Berlin-based vehicle operating as an international forum for post-Western alternative-civilization advocacy; advocacy of Orthodox-Russian-traditionalist framework; cooperation with WCF including Yakunin participation in multiple WCF Congresses per analyst reporting. Yakunin's position post-2014: EU and Canadian sanctions on Yakunin 2014; consequences including Yakunin son taking British citizenship producing Russian-state criticism. Yakunin's position post-2022: continuing operation of Dialogue of Civilizations from Berlin; positioning on Russian-Ukrainian war framing aligned with Russian state positions.

**The Russkiy Mir Foundation.** Founded 21 June 2007 by Putin presidential decree as vehicle of Russian-language and Russian-cultural promotion globally. The architecture: headquarters in Moscow; executive director Vladimir Kochin from post-2014 period; global presence through Russkiy Mir Centers in over 100 countries including presence in Eastern European countries, former Soviet states, Western European countries, and Asia, Africa, and the Americas; cooperation with Moscow Patriarchate including joint programming on "Russian world" (*Russkiy Mir*) framework. The consequences post-2022: closures of multiple Russkiy Mir Centers across Western European and Eastern European countries following Russian invasion of Ukraine; reorganisation of international operations including pivot toward BRICS and Global South operations.

**The World Russian People's Council (Vsemirnyy Russkiy Narodnyy Sobor).** Founded 1993 as Moscow Patriarchate-affiliated body operating with position on "traditional values", "Russian world" doctrine, and positions supporting Russian military operations in Ukraine from 2022. The architecture: headquarters in Moscow; Patriarch Kirill's involvement as honorary co-chair; annual gatherings including the World Russian People's Council 25th anniversary 2018 and 30th anniversary 2023; position on "Russian world" doctrine per statements including the 2023 statement framing Russian operations in Ukraine as "holy war" defending Russian Orthodox civilizational values. The position is illustration of Russian Orthodox-state integration producing ideological-religious framing of Russian foreign policy operations.

**The St Petersburg International Economic Forum (SPIEF).** Operates as annual venue for Russian-Orthodox-aligned networking from 1997; venue for annual Russian-state convening of foreign business and political figures; locations for informal meetings between Russian Orthodox-aligned figures and international counterparts including WCF, ADF International, and other Christian Right figures per investigative reporting. The architecture operates with consequences post-2022: Western withdrawal from SPIEF participation; continuing BRICS and Global South participation.

**The Russian-Christian Right cooperation.** Per the Bureau of Investigative Journalism September 2022 reporting: "unholy alliance" of US Christian Right, militant European Catholics, Russian Orthodox hardliners, and sanctioned oligarchs operating in a concerted campaign to undermine reproductive and LGBTQ rights globally. The architecture: Konstantin Malofeev's financial relationships with WCF; Aleksey Komov (WCF coordinator for Russia) cooperation with Brian Brown (WCF President); the Bureau of Investigative Journalism report findings on "transnational opposition to reproductive rights" pattern; Brian Brown response characterising the characterisation as "made up garbage and lies" per Bureau reporting. The architecture is illustration of Christian Right-Russian Orthodox cooperation producing joint advocacy on overlapping policy objectives — operating despite sustained Russian-Western geopolitical conflict post-2022.

The summary of § II: the Russian Orthodox international coordination architecture operates through the Moscow Patriarchate (cross-reference Chapter 17), the Konstantin Malofeev financial network, the Alexander Dugin ideological framework, the Vladimir Yakunin networking vehicle (Dialogue of Civilizations), the Russkiy Mir Foundation (founded 21 June 2007), and the World Russian People's Council (founded 1993). The configuration operates with cooperation across confessional boundaries — particularly cooperation with US Christian Right vehicles (WCF, ADF International, CitizenGO) on overlapping policy objectives. The architecture operates despite sustained Russian-Western geopolitical conflict post-2022 — producing illustration of cross-tradition reactionary cooperation continuing across international rupture lines. The Catholic-Evangelical-Orthodox cooperation dossiered in § III is the expression of the cross-confessional pattern.

---


### III. Catholic-Evangelical-Orthodox Cooperation: The "Ecumenism of Hate"

The Catholic-Evangelical-Orthodox cooperation is the principal trans-confessional Christian cooperation pattern of the contemporary period. The architecture has five principal nodes: (1) the post-1994 "Evangelicals and Catholics Together" dialogue in the United States. (2) the Spadaro-Figueroa La Civiltà Cattolica July 2017 "ecumenism of hate" framing as the Vatican-adjacent analytical articulation of the cooperation pattern. (3) the February 2016 Havana meeting between Pope Francis and Patriarch Kirill as the Catholic-Russian Orthodox meeting and the post-2022 rupture. (4) the Catholic Integralist current including Adrian Vermeule and Patrick Deneen vehicles. (5) the Christian nationalist coordination across US, European, Latin American, and post-Soviet terrain.

**The "Evangelicals and Catholics Together" dialogue.** Established 1994 by Charles Colson (1931-2012, Watergate-era figure and Prison Fellowship founder) and Richard John Neuhaus (1936-2009, Catholic priest and *First Things* editor). The architecture: dialogue producing joint statements on theological-political topics including the 1994 founding statement "Evangelicals and Catholics Together: The Christian Mission in the Third Millennium"; 1997 statement "The Gift of Salvation"; 2002 statement "Your Word Is Truth"; theological-political cooperation on anti-abortion advocacy, religious-liberty advocacy, and anti-same-sex-marriage advocacy since the post-1994 US period. The architecture is illustration of post-1980s reconfiguration of US Catholic-Evangelical relations — moving from historical Catholic-Evangelical antagonism (Protestant suspicion of Roman Catholic positions across centuries) toward cooperation on overlapping political-cultural objectives despite continuing theological-doctrinal differences.

**The post-1994 US Catholic-Evangelical political cooperation.** Cross-reference Chapter 16. The architecture: Catholic-Evangelical cooperation on anti-abortion advocacy operating through vehicles including the National Right to Life Committee (founded 1968), the Susan B. Anthony List (founded 1992), Americans United for Life, the March for Life (annual since 1974), and Operation Rescue (founded 1986); Catholic-Evangelical cooperation on anti-same-sex-marriage advocacy operating through the National Organization for Marriage (founded 2007), the Heritage Foundation, the Family Research Council; Catholic-Evangelical cooperation on religious-liberty advocacy operating through the Alliance Defending Freedom (cross-reference § I) and the Becket Fund for Religious Liberty (founded 1994). The architecture operates as consequence of post-Roe v Wade (1973) reconfiguration of US Catholic-Evangelical relations producing alliance against the post-1973 liberal political-legal framework.

**The Spadaro-Figueroa La Civiltà Cattolica July 2017 article.** Published 13 July 2017 in *La Civiltà Cattolica* (the Jesuit periodical with Vatican Secretariat of State pre-publication review). The architecture: article authors Antonio Spadaro SJ (then *La Civiltà Cattolica* editor-in-chief) and Marcelo Figueroa (Presbyterian editor of the Argentine edition of *L'Osservatore Romano* and personal friend of Pope Francis); article title "Evangelical Fundamentalism and Catholic Integralism in the USA: A Surprising Ecumenism"; thesis: US Catholic-Evangelical cooperation is the "ecumenism of hate" — alliance based not on shared theological commitments but on shared opposition to "enemies" including Muslims, LGBTQ persons, reproductive rights advocates, and secular frameworks. The consequences: response from US Catholic figures including critics framing the article as Vatican-aligned attack on the US Catholic-Evangelical alliance; response from evangelical-Catholic figures including framing of the article as misunderstanding of the US Christian Right configuration. The article operates as Vatican-adjacent analytical articulation of the Catholic-Evangelical cooperation pattern dossiered in this section — and as institutional illustration of institutional Francis-Vatican positioning against reactionary US Christian Right vehicles.

**The February 2016 Havana meeting between Pope Francis and Patriarch Kirill.** Conducted 12 February 2016 at the José Martí International Airport in Havana, Cuba. The architecture: the first-ever meeting between a Pope and a Russian Orthodox Patriarch in the history of the post-1054 East-West Christian schism; joint declaration signed by Francis and Kirill including joint positions on Christian persecution in Middle East (post-2014 Islamic State persecution of Christian communities), family questions, secularisation concerns, and bioethical questions; symbolic significance as moment of Catholic-Russian Orthodox reproachment after centuries of estrangement. The consequences: optimism in post-February 2016 period about possible rapprochement; rupture post-February 2022 following Patriarch Kirill support for Russian military operations in Ukraine — Pope Francis critique of Kirill as serving as "Putin's altar boy" in Reuters interview May 2022; consequences including cooling of post-2016 dialogue.

**The Catholic Integralist current.** The architecture: contemporary Catholic Integralist current operates as theological-political programme advocating Catholic confessional state arrangements; the principal vehicles include Adrian Vermeule (Harvard Law Professor, post-2016 convert from Anglicanism to Catholicism, advocate of "common-good constitutionalism"), Patrick Deneen (Notre Dame Political Science Professor, author of *Why Liberalism Failed* 2018 and *Regime Change* 2023), Sohrab Ahmari (founder of *Compact Magazine* 2022), and Gladden Pappin (former University of Dallas faculty, President of the Hungarian Institute of International Affairs from 2023). The positions: Integralist advocacy for post-liberal political arrangements; critique of procedural-liberal framework; positioning aligned with Hungarian Orbán government model and other "illiberal democratic" vehicles. The architecture is illustration of post-Roe Catholic intellectual reorientation — movement from post-Vatican II liberal-Catholic positioning toward Integralist positioning aligned with Christian nationalist political trajectories.

**Christian nationalist coordination.** Cross-reference Chapter 16 § IV. The architecture: post-2014 ascendance of Christian nationalist vehicles in the United States including cooperation between Catholic Integralist, Evangelical Christian nationalist, Charismatic-Pentecostal Christian nationalist, and Eastern Orthodox traditionalist vehicles; shared positioning including advocacy for "Christian America" framework, opposition to LGBTQ rights, opposition to reproductive rights, advocacy for religious-liberty framework operating in tension with secular anti-discrimination framework. The architecture is illustration of post-2014 trans-confessional Christian nationalist convergence on overlapping political-cultural objectives — operating despite sustained theological-doctrinal differences across confessional boundaries.

**Project 2025.** Published April 2023 by the Heritage Foundation as the blueprint for Trump second-term governance. The architecture: a 920-page document with contributions from 350+ contributors including figures from Christian nationalist, Catholic Integralist, the Family Research Council, the Heritage Foundation, ADF, and other Christian Right vehicles; policy programme including restructuring of federal executive agencies, positioning on immigration restriction, positioning on LGBTQ rights restriction, positioning on reproductive rights restriction, and positioning on "religious liberty" framework expansion. Project 2025 is illustration of contemporary Catholic-Evangelical cooperation operating through policy-document vehicle — consolidation of trans-confessional Christian Right priorities into a unified governance programme for Trump second-term implementation.

**The pattern of "ecumenism of hate".** The architecture: the Spadaro-Figueroa thesis operates as analytical articulation of the pattern observed across the § I and § II configurations — cooperation between Christians of different confessional backgrounds and cooperation between Christians and non-Christian traditionalist vehicles (OIC cooperation on "family values" advocacy at UN level, cooperation between Hindu nationalist Sangh Parivar vehicles and US Christian Right vehicles per Diplomat / TRT analysis, etc) operating on the basis of shared opposition to liberal-secular framework rather than shared theological commitments. The architecture operates as institutional illustration of contemporary trans-tradition reactionary cooperation under "traditional values" banner — operating despite theological incompatibilities across confessional boundaries.

The summary of § III: the Catholic-Evangelical-Orthodox cooperation serves as the principal trans-confessional Christian cooperation pattern of the contemporary period. The configuration operates through post-1994 Evangelicals and Catholics Together dialogue; Spadaro-Figueroa La Civiltà Cattolica July 2017 "ecumenism of hate" articulation; the February 2016 Havana Francis-Kirill meeting and post-2022 rupture; Catholic Integralist current including Vermeule, Deneen, Ahmari, and Pappin; Christian nationalist coordination across US, European, Latin American, and post-Soviet terrain; Project 2025 consolidation. The architecture is illustration of contemporary trans-confessional Christian reactionary cooperation — operating despite theological-doctrinal differences across confessional boundaries. The trans-religious coordination at UN, EU, OAS, and African Union levels dossiered in § IV is the expansion of the cross-confessional pattern beyond Christian institutional boundaries.

---


### IV. Trans-Religious Coordination at UN, EU, OAS, and African Union

The trans-religious coordination at international institutional level is the contemporary moment of the cross-tradition reactionary cooperation pattern dossiered in §§ I-III. The architecture has six principal nodes: (1) the Christian Right presence at the UN from post-1994 Cairo and post-1995 Beijing moments. (2) the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) as the vehicle of Muslim-majority state cooperation. (3) the Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam (5 August 1990) as the articulation of Sharia-bounded human-rights framework. (4) the Istanbul Process emerging from UN Human Rights Council Resolution 16/18 (March 2011). (5) the Mexico Declaration 2022 as the Latin American manifestation of cross-tradition coordination. (6) the African Union configuration on family values including the Uganda Anti-Homosexuality Act 2023.

**Christian Right presence at the UN from post-1994.** Per Political Research Associates analysis (Jennifer Butler 2000): the post-1994 Cairo International Conference on Population and Development and the post-1995 Beijing World Conference on Women produced reactionary mobilisation by coalition of Conservative Catholics, Mormons, Conservative Evangelicals, and "to a much smaller degree, Muslims and Jews" — operating to "delay and where possible derail progressive change" obtained through UN conferences and treaties. The architecture: coalition operating through Vatican position at UN (the Holy See holds Permanent Observer status at the UN), Mormon positions at UN, Evangelical vehicles at UN, and selective Muslim-majority state cooperation; position against expansion of "sexual and reproductive health rights" language at UN level; position against LGBTQ rights language at UN level; advocacy for "natural family" language at UN level. The architecture is illustration of trans-confessional reactionary cooperation at UN level emerging from Cairo-Beijing 1994-1995 context.

**The Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC).** Founded 25 September 1969 (Charter signed); formally established 1971 in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia. The architecture: OIC origins in the 1969 Rabat Summit (convened in response to the 21 August 1969 arson attack on the Al-Aqsa Mosque) and the 1970 Jeddah meeting of Muslim foreign ministers; 57 member states across four continents including Saudi Arabia, Iran, Pakistan, Indonesia, Egypt, Turkey, and the Palestinian Authority; headquarters in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia; Secretary-General Hissein Brahim Taha; structure including the Islamic Summit Conference (convened every three years), the Council of Foreign Ministers (annual), the General Secretariat, the Al-Quds Committee, the Islamic Development Bank, the Islamic Solidarity Fund, and the Islamic Educational Scientific and Cultural Organization (ISESCO). The architecture: OIC 2018 estimated population 1.81 billion; OIC 2019 estimated GDP (PPP) $27.949 trillion; second-largest intergovernmental organisation after the UN.

**The OIC-2025 Programme of Action.** Per OIC documents: the OIC-2025 programme adopted 2015 covering the 2016-2025 decade with the 18 priority areas and 107 goals; priority areas including peace and security, the Palestinian issue, counter-terrorism, economic cooperation, education, science and technology, climate change, women and family issues, and Islamophobia. The architecture operates as vehicle of Muslim-majority state cooperation on overlapping policy objectives — operating across significant theological, political, and economic differences between member states (Sunni-Shia differences cross-reference Chapters 5/18/19; differences between monarchic and republican member states; differences between authoritarian and less-authoritarian member states).

**The authoritarian governance of OIC member states.** Per Freedom House Freedom in the World 2024 report and Grokipedia analysis January 2026: over 80% of the 57 OIC member states classified as "Not Free" including Saudi Arabia (Freedom House score 8/100), Iran (11/100), and Egypt (18/100); predominance of authoritarian governance correlates with limited political pluralism and civil liberties suppression; consequence that OIC positions reflect preferences of authoritarian member-state regimes rather than democratic-electoral preferences of Muslim-majority populations. The architecture is illustration of Muslim-majority state configuration — operating distinct from Christian Right Western configurations dossiered in §§ I-III, but producing alignment with Christian Right vehicles on overlapping policy objectives including opposition to LGBTQ rights, opposition to expansion of reproductive rights language at the international level, and opposition to secular human-rights framework.

**The Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam (5 August 1990).** Adopted by 45 OIC foreign ministers on 5 August 1990 in Cairo. The architecture: articulation of a human-rights framework operating "in as much as they are compatible with Sharia (Quranic Law)"; a document presented as alternative or supplement to the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR, 1948); positions including subordination of human-rights provisions to Sharia provisions, restrictions on religious freedom (the Cairo Declaration does not include a right to change religion or to leave Islam), positions on women's rights subordinated to Sharia framework, and positions on family questions. The architecture operates as manifestation of Muslim-majority state cooperation on religious-state framework — and operates analogous to Christian positions on Sharia-vs-UDHR question (with Catholic Holy See positions, Mormon positions, and Evangelical positions operating in opposition to full secular UDHR framework on similar grounds of religious-priority over secular framework).

**The Istanbul Process and UN Human Rights Council Resolution 16/18.** The architecture: UN Human Rights Council Resolution 16/18 adopted March 2011 on "freedom of expression and incitement to religious hatred" — a consensus resolution emerging from negotiations between OIC and Western states; the Istanbul Process initiative emerging from Resolution 16/18 with annual meetings examining implementation; positions including position that "freedom of speech/expression" should be exercised to not "incite hatred, discrimination and violence based on one's religion or beliefs". The architecture is illustration of negotiation between OIC preferences for anti-blasphemy protection and Western preferences for broader free-speech framework — producing a compromise that OIC figures interpret as acceptance of limitations on anti-religious speech and that Western figures interpret as preservation of broader free-speech framework.

**The Mexico Declaration 2022.** Cross-reference § I. The architecture: declaration signed by the 670 lawmakers from the 18 Latin American countries; opposition to OAS and Inter-American Commission on Human Rights resolutions on abortion-related issues, right-to-life, family, and freedom-of-expression questions; ADF financial support per Ciper Chile investigative reporting; the Political Network for Values vehicle. The architecture operates as Latin American manifestation of cross-tradition coordination — cooperation between Catholic, Evangelical, and Mormon vehicles producing coordinated parliamentary-declaration against the regional human-rights framework. The consequences: influence on Latin American legislative-judicial configurations; contribution to post-2022 Latin American reactionary trajectories including the December 2025 Chile election outcome (cross-reference § I).

**The Uganda Anti-Homosexuality Act 2023 and the African Union configuration.** The architecture: the Uganda Anti-Homosexuality Act signed by President Yoweri Museveni on 29 May 2023, providing the death penalty for "aggravated homosexuality" provisions including HIV-positive same-sex activity, same-sex activity with minors, "serial offenders", and life-imprisonment provisions for other same-sex activity; Uganda's Constitutional Court partial decision April 2024 upholding most of the law; involvement of US Christian Right legal organisations including ADF International advocacy per Global Project Against Hate and Extremism reporting; WCF involvement per investigative reporting. The architecture is illustration of an African manifestation of cross-tradition coordination — cooperation between US Christian Right vehicles and Ugandan vehicles (Pentecostal-Evangelical vehicles operating with influence within Ugandan political-configuration) producing a legislative outcome harmful to LGBTQ persons in Uganda and a precedent for similar legislative action across other African countries.

**The contemporary 2026 cross-tradition coordination architecture.** As of late 2026, the cross-tradition coordination architecture operates as: (1) the WCF/ADF/CitizenGO network operating across 90+ countries with cooperation across Catholic, Evangelical, Mormon, Russian Orthodox, and selective Muslim-majority state vehicles; (2) the OIC vehicle operating with authoritarian-majority membership and positions aligned with Christian Right positions on overlapping policy objectives; (3) the cooperation at UN, EU, OAS, and African Union levels operating through diplomatic-cooperation, joint declarations, coordinated voting blocs, and financial-advocacy cooperation; (4) the pattern of "ecumenism of hate" operating across confessional boundaries on shared opposition to liberal-secular human-rights framework; (5) the consequences including the Uganda Anti-Homosexuality Act 2023, the Mexico Declaration 2022, the December 2025 Chile election outcome, and multiple other outcomes harmful to LGBTQ persons, reproductive-rights advocates, and liberal-secular figures globally.

The summary of § IV: the trans-religious coordination at international institutional level is the contemporary moment of cross-tradition reactionary cooperation. The configuration operates through Christian Right presence at the UN from post-1994 moments. OIC vehicle with 57 member states and positions aligned with Christian Right positions on overlapping policy objectives. The Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam (5 August 1990) as articulation of Sharia-bounded human-rights framework; the Istanbul Process emerging from UN HRC Resolution 16/18 (March 2011); the Mexico Declaration 2022 as Latin American manifestation; the Uganda Anti-Homosexuality Act 2023 as African manifestation. The architecture is illustration of contemporary trans-tradition reactionary cooperation under "traditional values" banner — operating despite theological incompatibilities and sustained geopolitical conflicts across boundaries. The section closes here. Chapter 29 dossiers the technocratic-religious interface — cooperation between captured-religious vehicles and contemporary technocratic-corporate figures including Silicon Valley figures, AI-development vehicles, and post-2024 configurations.

---


## Chapter 29. The Technocratic-Religious Interface

Chapter 29 dossiers the interface between contemporary technocratic-corporate vehicles and captured-religious vehicles. Where Chapter 28 dossiered cross-tradition coordination architecture (Christian Right international networks, Russian Orthodox international networks, Catholic-Evangelical-Orthodox cooperation, trans-religious UN/OIC coordination). Chapter 29 dossiers a parallel but distinct configuration: the interface through which Silicon Valley billionaire networks, AI-development corporate vehicles, and adjacent technocratic-intellectual currents have entered into operational cooperation with captured-religious vehicles in the 2014-2026 period. The configuration is not reducible to the cross-tradition cooperation dossiered in Chapter 28; it operates on distinct terrain through which technocratic-corporate capital, AI-development vehicles, neoreactionary intellectual currents, and Catholic Integralist and Christian nationalist political-theological currents have converged in producing the contemporary post-2024 configuration in the United States.

The record covers four sections: § I the Silicon Valley religious turn 2020-2026 including Peter Thiel's Antichrist lecture series September-October 2025, the ACTS 17 Collective vehicle, the Hallow Catholic prayer app positioning in Silicon Valley. And the broader "Christ-curious" tech-elite turn covered by the New York Times February 2025 and Religion News Service 2025-2026. § II JD Vance, the PayPal Mafia cluster, and Catholic Integralism in power including Vance's 2019 Catholic conversion. Thiel's $15M sponsorship of Vance's 2022 Senate campaign, the Trump second-term Cabinet's Catholic representation, and the contemporary integration of Catholic Integralist intellectual currents with executive-branch power. § III Curtis Yarvin, neoreaction. And the "Dark Enlightenment" including Yarvin's theoretical articulation of "the Cathedral", monarchist-CEO governance framework, and influence on Vance, Thiel, Andreessen, and Anton. § IV the AI-development terrain as secular-religion configuration including longtermism. Effective altruism, rationalism, the networks around OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepMind, and xAI, the TESCREAL framework critique, and the patterns through which AI-development corporate vehicles have themselves taken on characteristics of religious-veneration discourse.

The evidence operates with the same investigative-journalism standards and the same captured-state analytic framework applied across the preceding chapters. The analysis operates against any framing that would treat the Silicon Valley technocratic configuration as secular in opposition to captured-religious configurations dossiered elsewhere; the contemporary technocratic-religious interface dossiered here operates as a distinct zone of cooperation in which technocratic-corporate capital and captured-religious vehicles have converged in mutually-reinforcing partnership.

### I. The Silicon Valley Religious Turn 2020-2026

The contemporary Silicon Valley religious turn is the moment in which a section of Silicon Valley technocratic-billionaire networks has openly entered into Christian participation, advocacy, and intellectual production. The architecture has five principal nodes: (1) Peter Thiel as the principal billionaire figure articulating Christian theological-political framework in Silicon Valley. (2) the ACTS 17 Collective as the principal vehicle of Silicon Valley Christian convening. (3) Hallow and "He Gets Us" as vehicles of Christian tech-product and advertising mobilisation. (4) mainstream-media recognition of the Silicon Valley religious turn including the New York Times February 2025 feature and the Washington Post October 2025 reporting. (5) public-intellectual critique including Episcopal and Catholic responses to the Thiel Antichrist lectures.

**Peter Thiel and the Antichrist lecture series.** Peter Andreas Thiel (born 11 October 1967 Frankfurt) — PayPal co-founder, Palantir Technologies co-founder, Founders Fund principal — is the principal contemporary Silicon Valley billionaire articulating Christian theological-political framework. The architecture: Thiel self-describes as heterodox Christian; intellectual influence from René Girard (Stanford French Studies 1923-2015) who provided "a basis for his Christian faith without the fundamentalism of his parents" per Religion News reporting February 2025; Thiel public Christian theological discourse from podcast appearances 2023-2025; Thiel Antichrist lecture series September-October 2025 at the Commonwealth Club San Francisco — four sold-out two-hour lectures titled "The Antichrist: A Four-Part Lecture Series" hosted by the ACTS 17 Collective; thesis: authoritarian agents disguise themselves as bearers of safety and security, taking advantage of human vulnerability to surrender individual liberties for promised survival; framing of "AI critics" as potential "legionnaires of the Antichrist" for slowing technological progress that could counter existential threats. The lecture series is the moment at which Thiel's previously-private theological discourse entered into public visibility — generating Washington Post coverage by Nitasha Tiku October 2025, Religion News coverage January 2026, and public-intellectual criticism from Episcopal priest Kevin Deal (San Francisco) and others framing the lectures as "antithetical" to the Gospel.

**The ACTS 17 Collective.** Acknowledging Christ in Technology and Society — 501(c)(3) nonprofit organisation founded around 2023 by Michelle Stephens with co-founder Trae Stephens (Founders Fund partner, Anduril co-founder); positioning as Christian-tech convening vehicle in Silicon Valley. The architecture: events featuring tech-elite Christian figures; fundraising and tech-network cultivation; hosting of the Thiel Antichrist lecture series September-October 2025; cultivation of younger tech-elite Christian conversion and participation per Religion News reporting January 2026. The ACTS 17 Collective serves as the principal contemporary vehicle of Silicon Valley Christian convening — distinct from but cooperatively linked to the Hallow prayer-app ecosystem and the broader US Christian Right ecosystem dossiered in Chapter 28.

**Trae Stephens and the "God's command" framing.** Trae Stephens — Founders Fund partner, Anduril Industries co-founder (defence technology company founded 2017 by Palmer Luckey, Brian Schimpf, Matt Grimm, Trae Stephens, Joe Chen) — operates as bridge figure between Silicon Valley tech-elite and Christian advocacy. The architecture: Wired magazine reporting on Stephens explicitly aligning his technocratic ambitions with Christianity, arguing his work is critical "to carry out God's command to bring his kingdom to Earth as it is in heaven" per Armor of Truth analysis. The Stephens framing is illustration of the Christian theological framing of technology-development work in contemporary Silicon Valley — a framework operating adjacent to the broader Christian nationalist intellectual current dossiered in § II and Chapter 28 § III.

**Hallow.** Roman Catholic prayer app launched 2018 by Alex Jones (not the conspiracy theorist), Alessandro DiSanto, and Erich Kerekes; venture-capital backing; the "He Gets Us" advertising-campaign cross-promotion; popularity in Silicon Valley per multiple analyses. The architecture: Hallow operates as bridge between Catholic devotional practice and Silicon Valley product-development culture; growth throughout the post-2020 period; integration with broader US Catholic networks and positioning within the Catholic Integralist intellectual ecosystem dossiered in § II.

**The "He Gets Us" campaign.** A Christian advertising campaign launched March 2022; funding through The Servant Foundation (linked to Hobby Lobby co-founder David Green family); Super Bowl LVII February 2023 advertisement placement at a cost over $20 million; Super Bowl LVIII February 2024 advertisement placement; positioning of broad-inclusive Christian rebranding aligned with technocratic-Christian cooperation pattern. The architecture is illustration of contemporary US-Christian advertising-and-cultural campaign methodology — operating adjacent to Catholic Integralist intellectual current and the evangelical-Catholic cooperation pattern dossiered in Chapter 28 § III.

**The mainstream-media recognition of the Silicon Valley religious turn.** Emma Goldberg's "Seeking God, or Peter Thiel, in Silicon Valley" (*The New York Times*, 11 February 2025) is the principal mainstream-media recognition of the contemporary Silicon Valley religious turn. The architecture: Goldberg pattern of Silicon Valley tech-elite Christian conversion, ACTS 17 Collective growth, and turn from "secular citadel of detached rationalism" framing toward Christian-tech cultural-ideological hybrid per Daniel Pinchbeck Liminal News December 2025 analysis. Nitasha Tiku's Washington Post reporting on Thiel Antichrist lectures October 2025 is the mainstream-media follow-up. The Religion News Service 2025-2026 reporting cluster including the January 2026 piece on "Tech Revival After Peter Thiel's Antichrist Talks" is continuing coverage.

**Critical responses: Father Kevin Deal and Father Seraphim Rose.** Rev. Kevin Deal — priest at progressive LGBTQ-affirming Episcopal church in San Francisco — public critique of Thiel Antichrist lectures October 2025 framing them as "antithetical" to the Gospel and as "centered on Peter Thiel and his kind of apocalyptic cynicism" per Religion News reporting. Father Seraphim Rose (1934-1982) — Orthodox Christian writer; St Herman of Alaska Monastery north of San Francisco; "Orthodoxy and the Religion of the Future" — influence on young male Internet-savvy converts to Orthodox Christianity per Religion Unplugged February 2025 analysis. The architecture is illustration of the cross-confessional Christian critical response to the contemporary Silicon Valley Christian turn — Episcopal critique from the progressive end, Orthodox critique from the traditional-monastic end, Catholic critique from Pope Francis position against the "ecumenism of hate" (cross-reference Chapter 28 § III).

**The Silicon Valley religious turn as pattern.** As of mid-2026, the contemporary Silicon Valley religious turn operates as: (1) moment of Christian theological-political articulation by tech-elite billionaire figures including Peter Thiel and Trae Stephens; (2) growth of Christian-tech convening vehicles including ACTS 17 Collective and the Hallow prayer-app ecosystem; (3) mainstream-media recognition through NYT, Washington Post, and Religion News Service reporting clusters 2025-2026; (4) cooperation with broader US Christian Right ecosystem and Catholic Integralist intellectual current; (5) critical responses from Episcopal, Catholic, and Orthodox Christian figures framing the Silicon Valley Christian turn as theologically heterodox and politically reactionary.

The summary of § I: the Silicon Valley religious turn 2020-2026 is the moment in which Silicon Valley technocratic-billionaire networks have entered into open Christian participation. The configuration operates through Thiel's Antichrist lectures September-October 2025, the ACTS 17 Collective vehicle, the Hallow Catholic prayer app, the "He Gets Us" advertising campaign, and mainstream-media recognition. The architecture provides the terrain on which JD Vance and the Catholic Integralist current dossiered in § II have entered into political-executive power — and on which the Curtis Yarvin neoreactionary intellectual current dossiered in § III has gained influence. The architecture provides context for the AI-development corporate-religious interface dossiered in § IV.

---


### II. JD Vance, the PayPal Mafia, and Catholic Integralism in Power

The contemporary integration of Catholic Integralist intellectual current with executive-branch political power is the institutional achievement of the post-November 2024 US configuration. The architecture has six principal nodes: (1) JD Vance's 2019 conversion to Roman Catholicism and trajectory to the Vice Presidency from 20 January 2025. (2) Peter Thiel's sponsorship of Vance's 2022 Senate campaign with $15 million. (3) the PayPal Mafia cluster operating across multiple tech-corporate and political vehicles. (4) the Trump second-term Cabinet's Catholic representation including Rubio. Kennedy, Duffy, McMahon, and Vance. (5) the Catholic Integralist intellectual current cross-referenced from Chapter 28 § III now adjacent to executive power; (6) the Pope Francis critique of Vance's Catholic articulations and the post-Francis contestation around US Catholic identity.

**JD Vance and the 2019 Catholic conversion.** James David Vance — born 2 August 1984 Middletown Ohio — serves as the principal contemporary Catholic Integralist political figure. The architecture: Vance Yale Law School degree 2013; conversion to Roman Catholicism 2019 with confirmation at St Gertrude Priory (Dominican) in Cincinnati; author of *Hillbilly Elegy* (2016); Senate election Ohio 2022; Vice Presidential selection by Donald Trump July 2024; Vice Presidential inauguration 20 January 2025. The architecture operates with implications: Vance Catholic Integralist intellectual positioning per Salon February 2026 analysis; Vance position aligned with Adrian Vermeule's "common-good constitutionalism" framework and Patrick Deneen's post-liberal political-theological framework (cross-reference Chapter 28 § III); Vance appearance at Lance Wallnau-hosted Christian nationalist event September 2024 per PBS NewsHour reporting; Vance speech at March for Life 2025 combining pro-life messaging with Catholic-influenced new-populism rhetoric.

**Thiel's $15 million sponsorship of Vance's 2022 Senate campaign.** Per Robert Reich substack analysis and multiple investigative reports: Thiel committed $15 million to Vance's 2022 Senate campaign through the Protect Ohio Values super PAC; Thiel institutional introduction of Vance to Trump at Mar-a-Lago early 2021 to convince Trump that Vance had become a Trump loyalist per Robert Reich analysis. The architecture is evidence of Silicon Valley billionaire capacity to engineer political careers — and as illustration of the contemporary PayPal Mafia cluster's translation into executive-branch political influence. The pattern: Thiel sponsorship → Vance Senate campaign 2022 → Vance Vice Presidential selection July 2024 → Vance position as MAGA successor per Wall Street Journal August 2025 reporting.

**The PayPal Mafia cluster.** Operates as the cluster of figures with PayPal institutional origins who have entered into contemporary tech-political institutional influence. The architecture: Peter Thiel (PayPal co-founder, Founders Fund, Palantir co-founder); Elon Musk (PayPal co-founder via X.com merger, Tesla, SpaceX, X Corp, xAI, DOGE); Reid Hoffman (PayPal early member, LinkedIn co-founder, Greylock Partners); Max Levchin (PayPal co-founder, Affirm); David Sacks (PayPal early member, Yammer founder, Craft Ventures, Trump White House crypto-and-AI czar from January 2025); Keith Rabois (PayPal early member, Khosla Ventures, Founders Fund); Roelof Botha (PayPal early member, Sequoia Capital). The pattern: overlapping involvement in Founders Fund, Anduril, Palantir, and venture-capital networks producing capacity for coordinated political-financial influence per Robert Reich analysis and multiple other reports. The PayPal Mafia operates as cluster providing the financial-organisational architecture of the contemporary Silicon Valley-political integration dossiered across this chapter.

**Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).** Elon Reeve Musk — born 28 June 1971 Pretoria South Africa — is the principal figure of contemporary tech-political integration. The architecture: $295 million Musk contribution to Trump and Republican 2024 campaigns per New York Post analysis; Musk appointment as Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) head from January 2025; DOGE dismantling of USAID, Department of Education, and multiple other federal agencies January-May 2025; Musk-Trump public falling-out May-June 2025; Musk pivot to support Vance for 2028 per Wall Street Journal August 2025 reporting; Musk announcement of "America Party" intention July 2025 followed by decision to suspend party plans August 2025 to maintain Vance institutional relationship. The architecture is illustration of the contemporary technocratic-political integration — and as pattern through which Silicon Valley billionaire capital is operating on executive-branch governance directly rather than through traditional political-party institutional channels.

**The Trump second-term Cabinet Catholic representation.** Per Religion News Service February 2025 analysis: Cabinet-level Catholic representation including JD Vance (Vice President, Catholic convert 2019), Marco Rubio (Secretary of State, cradle Catholic), Robert F. Kennedy Jr (HHS Secretary, cradle Catholic), Sean Duffy (Transportation Secretary, cradle Catholic), Linda McMahon (Education Secretary, Catholic convert). The pattern: majority-Catholic representation in Trump second-term Cabinet — distinctive in US political history given historical Protestant dominance of executive-branch positions; consequence that contemporary US executive-branch Catholic representation does not produce policy alignment with Catholic social teaching per America Magazine February 2025 analysis and Religion News Service analysis. The institutional contradiction: Catholic-Cabinet representation operating alongside attacks on Catholic Relief Services, Catholic Charities, and USAID dismantling that undermines Catholic-affiliated humanitarian vehicles.

**Catholic Integralism as intellectual current.** Cross-reference Chapter 28 § III. The architecture: Catholic Integralism operates as theological-political framework advocating Catholic confessional state arrangements; principal figures include Adrian Vermeule (Harvard Law, advocate of "common-good constitutionalism"), Patrick Deneen (Notre Dame, *Why Liberalism Failed* 2018, *Regime Change* 2023), Sohrab Ahmari (*Compact Magazine* founded 2022), Gladden Pappin (Hungarian Institute of International Affairs President from 2023); positions advocating post-liberal political institutional arrangements aligned with Hungarian Orbán government model. The contemporary position: Catholic Integralist intellectual current is adjacent to JD Vance's positioning — providing the theological-political framework within which Vance's executive-branch institutional role is articulated per multiple analyses including Catholic Culture, Salon, Religion News, and Institut Montaigne reporting.

**The "ordo amoris" controversy February 2025.** The architecture: Vance Fox News appearance January 2025 invoking the Catholic theological concept "*ordo amoris*" (ordering of love) — citing Augustinian and Thomistic theological framework — to justify Trump administration deportation policy prioritising citizens over non-citizens; Vance instruction to viewers to "Google ordo amoris" to understand the framework; Pope Francis response in February 2025 letter to US Bishops critiquing Vance's interpretation as misapplication of the theological concept and as instrumentalisation of Catholic theological language for anti-immigrant policy; Religion News Service February 2025 critical analysis. The architecture is illustration of the contemporary Catholic-contestation around Vance's theological articulations — and as moment of Francis-Vance contestation that prefigured the uncertainty following Francis's April 2025 death (cross-reference Chapter 15 for Vatican succession context).

**The post-Francis contestation.** The architecture: Pope Francis death 21 April 2025; papal conclave May 2025 election of Cardinal Robert Francis Prevost (born 14 September 1955 Chicago) as Pope Leo XIV — the first US-born Pope; Leo XIV positioning under institutional observation regarding institutional continuity vs. discontinuity with Francis's framework; implications for US Catholic Integralist current including potential alignment or contestation with Vance-aligned Catholic Integralist current; uncertainty as of mid-2026 regarding Leo XIV's positioning on US Catholic Cabinet representation, Catholic Integralism, and the Vance-Francis "ordo amoris" framework dispute. The architecture operates as contemporary moment of contestation around US Catholic identity in the Trump second-term political configuration.

**The contemporary integration of Catholic Integralism with executive power.** As of mid-2026, the integration of Catholic Integralism with executive-branch power operates through: (1) JD Vance as Vice President (Catholic Integralist intellectual positioning); (2) the Trump second-term Cabinet Catholic representation (Vance, Rubio, Kennedy, Duffy, McMahon); (3) the Catholic Integralist intellectual current adjacency (Vermeule, Deneen, Ahmari, Pappin); (4) the Thiel and PayPal Mafia sponsorship architecture; (5) the Trump second-term policy programme aligned with Project 2025 (Heritage Foundation, cross-reference Chapter 28 § III); (6) the post-Francis Vatican contestation around US Catholic identity. The configuration produces contradictions including Cabinet-level Catholic representation operating alongside attacks on Catholic humanitarian vehicles, ascendance of Catholic Integralist intellectual current operating alongside Vatican ambivalence, and PayPal Mafia sponsorship of Catholic-articulated political figures operating alongside PayPal Mafia figures who themselves do not identify as Catholic (Musk, Andreessen, others).

The summary of § II: the contemporary integration of Catholic Integralist intellectual current with executive-branch political power is the structural achievement of the post-November 2024 US configuration. The configuration delivered: JD Vance Vice Presidency from 20 January 2025; Trump second-term Cabinet Catholic representation; PayPal Mafia sponsorship architecture; Catholic Integralist intellectual current adjacency to executive power; post-Francis Vatican contestation. The architecture operates alongside the Curtis Yarvin neoreactionary intellectual current dossiered in § III — and operates adjacent to the AI-development corporate-religious interface dossiered in § IV.

---


### III. Curtis Yarvin, Neoreaction, and the "Dark Enlightenment"

The Curtis Yarvin neoreactionary intellectual current is the principal contemporary techno-authoritarian intellectual framework articulating the technocratic-corporate turn against liberal-democratic governance. The architecture has six principal nodes: (1) Curtis Yarvin himself as the principal figure of contemporary neoreactionary theory. (2) the "Mencius Moldbug" pseudonymous corpus 2007-2014 articulating the foundational neoreactionary framework. (3) the "Cathedral" coinage as the principal critical framework against journalism-academia institutional alliance. (4) the monarchist-CEO governance framework advocating replacement of liberal-democratic governance with corporate-CEO autocratic arrangement. (5) the Thiel and PayPal Mafia sponsorship of Yarvin including investment in Tlon Corporation. (6) the Vance, Andreessen, Anton uptake of Yarvin's framework in contemporary executive-branch and tech-corporate institutional contexts.

**Curtis Yarvin and the Mencius Moldbug corpus.** Curtis Guy Yarvin — born 1973 — is the principal figure of contemporary neoreactionary intellectual production per Britannica's account. The architecture: Yarvin software-engineer background; pseudonymous blogging as Mencius Moldbug from 2007 on the blog "Unqualified Reservations"; foundational text "An Open Letter to Open-Minded Progressives" (April-July 2008, 120,000-word serial manifesto) arguing that egalitarianism is responsible for most of the world's ills and urging readers to take "the red pill" of ideological awakening per New Yorker analysis; foundational text "Patchwork: A Political System for the 21st Century" (2008) articulating fragmentation of nation-states into corporate-governance city-states; the 2013 TechCrunch article "Geeks for Monarchy" revealing the Mencius Moldbug identity as Curtis Yarvin; founding of Tlon Corporation around 2013 (named for Borges story "Tlön, Uqbar, Orbis Tertius"); the current Substack "Gray Mirror" with 49,000+ followers per Newsweek January 2025 analysis. The architecture is an intellectual production cluster from which the contemporary neoreactionary movement has emerged — and stands adjacent to the Silicon Valley billionaire networks dossiered in §§ I-II.

**"The Cathedral" coinage.** This is the principal Yarvin critical framework against the alliance Yarvin identifies as the operative governance authority in contemporary US institutional life. The architecture: Yarvin defines "the Cathedral" in his Gray Mirror Substack as "journalism plus academia — in other words, the intellectual institutions at the center of modern society, just as the Church was the intellectual institution at the center of medieval society" per Newsweek reporting; thesis that the United States is "not a democracy in a meaningful sense" because unpopular policies persist despite majority opposition due to the Cathedral institutional dominance per Britannica analysis; framework operating as theoretical predicate for the advocacy for replacement of liberal-democratic governance with monarchist-CEO governance arrangement.

**The monarchist-CEO governance framework.** This is Yarvin's principal positive theoretical proposal. The architecture: Yarvin advocacy for replacement of liberal-democratic governance with "monarchy" governance via CEO or dictator figure; "American Caesar" framing per Michael Anton podcast appearances; advocacy for what Yarvin terms "modified monarchy" reducing the monarchical concept to a tool for authoritarian consolidation per radical-monarchist critical analysis; framework operating adjacent to Balaji Srinivasan's *Network State* book (2022) articulating concept of corporate-governance city-states; Yarvin advocacy for Silicon Valley CEO figure as institutional "Augustus" replacing the US republic per Jacobin December 2022 critical analysis. The architecture is a theoretical framework providing intellectual articulation for the contemporary technocratic-corporate turn against liberal-democratic governance dossiered across this chapter.

**Thiel and PayPal Mafia sponsorship of Yarvin.** Per Max Chafkin's *The Contrarian: Peter Thiel and Silicon Valley's Pursuit of Power* (2021) and multiple subsequent reports: Yarvin is "house political philosopher" of Thiel's "budding movement" per Jacobin analysis. The architecture: Thiel financial investment in Yarvin's Tlon Corporation startup around 2013; Thiel intellectual patronage producing institutional platform for Yarvin's theoretical work; broader PayPal Mafia engagement with Yarvin's framework including Marc Andreessen involvement per On Point April 2025 analysis. The architecture is a sponsorship pattern through which Silicon Valley billionaire capital has cultivated and amplified Yarvin's anti-democratic intellectual framework — and through which Yarvin's framework has entered into mainstream intellectual visibility and institutional influence.

**JD Vance and Yarvin's institutional influence.** Per Newsweek January 2025 reporting and multiple subsequent analyses: Vance public references to Yarvin's ideas in interviews 2021-2024; Vance positioning aligned with Yarvin's framework on administrative-state-dismantling proposals. The architecture: Vance interview on Tucker Carlson's programme 2021 — "I think what Trump should do, if I was giving him one piece of advice: Fire every single midlevel bureaucrat, every civil servant in the administrative state, and replace them with our people. And when the courts stop you, stand before the country and say, 'The chief justice has made his ruling. Now let him enforce it.'" per Salon February 2025 reporting; implementation pattern through post-January 2025 DOGE operations dismantling federal workforce and federal agencies per multiple reports. The architecture is illustration of Yarvin's theoretical framework producing translation into executive-branch policy implementation.

**Michael Anton and Yarvin platforming.** Per Newsweek January 2025 reporting: Michael Anton — Trump second-term State Department appointee, author of "The Flight 93 Election" (2016) under pseudonym Publius Decius Mus — serves as Yarvin platforming figure including podcast hosting of Yarvin on "installing an American Caesar" episode per Newsweek reporting. The architecture is illustration of Yarvin's framework entering executive-branch adjacency through appointee figures with intellectual affinity to neoreactionary framework.

**The "Dark Enlightenment" or NRx movement.** This is the broader intellectual current within which Yarvin's framework has been embedded. The architecture: Nick Land (former Warwick University philosopher) theoretical contribution including "Dark Enlightenment" essays 2013; NRx (neoreaction) movement emergence from online intellectual networks 2007-2015; intellectual continuity with earlier anti-democratic intellectual currents including Hans-Hermann Hoppe's *Democracy: The God That Failed* (2001) and broader libertarian-paleoconservative intellectual currents; contemporary reach producing uptake across Silicon Valley tech-elite networks per CBC Ideas January 2026 analysis. The architecture is an intellectual ecosystem within which the contemporary technocratic-corporate turn against liberal-democratic governance has been theoretically articulated and elaborated.

**The Network State.** This is an adjacent theoretical framework. The architecture: Balaji Srinivasan as author of *The Network State: How To Start a New Country* (2022); Srinivasan positioning as Andreessen Horowitz-aligned tech-elite figure; institutional framework advocating fragmentation of nation-states into corporate-governance institutional arrangements; adjacency to Yarvin's Patchwork framework; uptake across Silicon Valley tech-elite networks. The Srinivasan framework stands adjacent to Yarvin's framework as illustration of contemporary tech-elite anti-democratic theoretical production.

**The contemporary position of the neoreactionary current.** As of mid-2026, the Curtis Yarvin neoreactionary intellectual current functions as: (1) intellectual framework producing theoretical articulation for the contemporary technocratic-corporate turn against liberal-democratic governance; (2) institutional influence on Vance, Anton, and other Trump second-term executive-branch figures; (3) sponsorship by Thiel and broader PayPal Mafia cluster; (4) adjacency to Catholic Integralist intellectual current dossiered in § II despite Yarvin's non-Catholic theological positioning; (5) adjacency to Silicon Valley Christian turn dossiered in § I despite institutional and theological tensions between Yarvin's framework and Christian theological commitments per critical analyses including the radical-monarchist critique of Yarvin's instrumentalisation of monarchical concept.

The summary of § III: the Curtis Yarvin neoreactionary intellectual current is the principal contemporary techno-authoritarian intellectual framework. The configuration operates through Yarvin's Mencius Moldbug pseudonymous corpus 2007-2014, the "Cathedral" critical framework, the monarchist-CEO governance proposal, the Tlon Corporation vehicle, the Thiel and PayPal Mafia sponsorship architecture, the Vance and Anton uptake, and the broader Dark Enlightenment / NRx intellectual ecosystem including Nick Land and Balaji Srinivasan. The architecture provides the theoretical predicate for the contemporary technocratic-corporate turn against liberal-democratic governance — operating alongside the Catholic Integralist intellectual current dossiered in § II in contradictory but cooperative configuration with Catholic theological framework. The AI-development corporate-religious interface dossiered in § IV is the contemporary terrain on which Yarvin's framework, the Catholic Integralist current, and the Silicon Valley billionaire networks all converge.

---


### IV. AI Ethics as Secular Religion: Longtermism, EA, and the AI-Development Corporate-Religious Interface

The AI-development corporate-religious interface is the contemporary terrain on which AI-development corporate vehicles, longtermist-effective-altruist intellectual currents, and rationalist online communities have produced patterns of religious-veneration discourse, eschatological framing, and salvation-narrative articulation around AI development. The architecture has six principal nodes: (1) Eliezer Yudkowsky's foundational role in articulating AI as object of eschatological-religious framing through LessWrong (founded 2009) and the Machine Intelligence Research Institute. (2) the Effective Altruism (EA) movement and the emergence of longtermism as ethical-philosophical framework. (3) the Sam Bankman-Fried FTX collapse November 2022 and the EA contestation. (4) the OpenAI. Anthropic, DeepMind, and xAI configurations operating with religious-veneration discourse patterns. (5) the Torres-Gebru "TESCREAL" critique framework articulating the techno-utopian intellectual cluster; (6) the Anthony Levandowski "Way of the Future" AI-deity church and the broader pattern of AI-veneration discourse.

**Eliezer Yudkowsky and the rationalist tradition.** Eliezer Shlomo Yudkowsky — born 1979 — is the principal founding figure of the contemporary rationalist online intellectual current. The architecture: Yudkowsky founding of LessWrong online rationalist community 2009; the Machine Intelligence Research Institute (MIRI, formerly Singularity Institute for Artificial Intelligence) vehicle co-founded by Yudkowsky 2000; Yudkowsky positioning on AI existential risk; Yudkowsky's April 2023 *Time* magazine essay calling for international moratorium on AI development including advocacy for "airstrikes on rogue data centers" producing controversy; Yudkowsky-led Sequences textual corpus producing foundational rationalist intellectual framework. The architecture operates as intellectual founding moment from which the contemporary AI-existential-risk discourse and the rationalist-EA-longtermist cluster have emerged per Religion News May 2026 analysis.

**The Effective Altruism (EA) movement.** Per Wikipedia and multiple analyses: EA emerged  around 2009 from Oxford University academic networks. The architecture: founders William MacAskill (Oxford philosopher) and Toby Ord (Oxford philosopher); vehicles including Giving What We Can (founded 2009 by Ord), Centre for Effective Altruism (founded 2012 by MacAskill and Ord), 80,000 Hours (career-advice vehicle founded 2012), GiveWell (founded 2007 by Holden Karnofsky and Elie Hassenfeld), Open Philanthropy (founded 2014 by Cari Tuna and Dustin Moskovitz, Facebook co-founder); EA Global conferences annual from 2013; EA framework advocating "evidence-based, reason-based" philanthropic decision-making producing grant-making patterns. The architecture operates as intellectual-philanthropic movement that produced the longtermist ethical framework dossiered below — and that has institutionally engaged the contemporary AI-development configurations dossiered below.

**Longtermism as an ethical framework.** Per William MacAskill's *What We Owe the Future* (2022) and broader longtermist intellectual corpus: longtermism articulates an ethical framework treating "positively influencing the long-term future" as "key moral priority of our time" per MacAskill's articulation. The architecture: Toby Ord's *The Precipice* (2020) articulating an existential-risk framework; MacAskill's *Doing Good Better* (2015) and *What We Owe the Future* (2022) articulating a longtermist framework; position treating future human and "post-human" digital populations as moral patients per Religion News analysis. The architecture is an ethical framework that critics including Émile P. Torres and Timnit Gebru  critique as "trillion-year-timescale" ethical framework that treats future humans — including "digital beings" — as "more valuable than those living now" per Religion News May 2026 analysis.

**The Sam Bankman-Fried FTX collapse November 2022.** Operates as crisis moment for the EA movement. The architecture: Sam Bankman-Fried (born 6 March 1992 Stanford California) founding of FTX cryptocurrency exchange 2019; Bankman-Fried positioning as "earning-to-give" EA-aligned philanthropic donor with multi-hundred-million-dollar donations to EA causes pre-November 2022; FTX collapse November 2022 with bankruptcy filing 11 November 2022; Bankman-Fried arrest December 2022; Bankman-Fried conviction November 2023 on seven federal counts including wire fraud, securities fraud, and conspiracy; Bankman-Fried sentence March 2024 to a 25-year prison term. The architecture operates as crisis moment producing EA contestation about the epistemic and ethical adequacy of EA framework — and as moment of mainstream-media recognition of the previously-obscure EA-longtermist cluster.

**The OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepMind, and xAI configurations.** Per multiple analyses: contemporary AI-development corporate vehicles have emerged from networks adjacent to the EA-rationalist intellectual cluster dossiered above. The architecture: OpenAI founded December 2015 by founders Sam Altman, Elon Musk, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever, John Schulman, Wojciech Zaremba with an initial $1 billion funding commitment; Musk departure 2018 over strategic disagreements; OpenAI for-profit restructuring 2019 through OpenAI LP vehicle; Microsoft partnership 2019-2023 with multi-billion-dollar investments; the November 2023 OpenAI board crisis in which Altman was briefly removed by board members including EA-aligned figures and then reinstated within five days per multiple reports; December 2023 board reconstitution.
